business.headline: Reliance Q4 profit slips to Rs 16,971 crore; Rs 6 dividend announcedReliance Industries posted consolidated net profit attributable to owners of Rs 16,971 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, down from Rs 19,407 crore a year earlier.

RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence; to seek winding up in courtThe action marks a dramatic escalation in regulatory steps against Paytm Payments Bank, which had already faced severe business restrictions over the past two years.

Is not using your credit card doing more harm than good?For many people, the safest way to avoid debt is simple, i.e., stop using credit cards altogether. No swipes, no bills, no worries. But could this seemingly smart move actually be harming your credit score?

How this 25-year old built a Rs 20 lakh portfolio in almost 3 yearsBuilding a Rs 20 lakh portfolio usually feels like a long-term goal. But a 25-year-old techie managed to do it in just about three years. The surprising part? His salary wasn't very high, so what did he do differently?

Bernstein flags job risk, subsidy burden in open letter to PM ModiThe letter, which revisits themes first raised in 2019, comes at a time when India has moved up global GDP rankings but continues to face challenges in employment, innovation and infrastructure.

Should you pause your SIP when markets fall? Here's the truth most investors missThe moment markets dip, doubt creeps in. That steady SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) you trusted now feels risky. Should you stop and play safe for a while? The answer isn't as obvious as it seems.

Infosys grants Rs 51.75 crore ESOPs to CEO, employees still await pay hikeThe ESOP approval comes after a strong quarterly performance by the company. Infosys reported a 20.9% rise in net profit to Rs 8,501 crore in the March quarter, while revenue grew 13.4%.

Explained: How Rs 5 lakh PF withdrawal after 3 years could cost you Rs 1.5 lakhWithdrawing your PF after switching jobs may seem like an easy financial move. But what many don't realise is that an early withdrawal can come with a surprisingly high tax cost, cutting a big chunk from your savings.
Why HCLTech shares are under pressure againThe fresh decline comes just days after the company's latest quarterly results disappointed the Street and triggered a sharp sell-off in the stock.

Received money, mutual funds or shares as gifts? Don't miss these ITR rulesGifts in the form of money or investments are quite common, but they come with tax rules that are often overlooked. Knowing what to declare in your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices later.

8th Pay Commission: Key meetings to begin in Delhi from April 28, more rounds plannedIn a notice dated April 24, 2026, the Commission said it has received a large number of requests from employee unions and associations for meetings scheduled between April 28 and April 30 in Delhi.

Infosys share price crashes after Q4 results: Why stock fell 6% todayThe IT giant's share price declined 6% intraday, making it one of the biggest losers in the technology pack, as investors reacted more to future guidance than to the quarterly profit itself.

AI security risk for banks? Why has Nirmala Sitharaman raised concernsAt the centre of the issue is Anthropic's new AI model, known as Claude Mythos Preview, capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers when prompted.

Infosys shares down 5%, hit 52-week low: Why IT stock tumbled after Q4 resultsAt around 11:01 am, Infosys shares were trading at Rs 1,178.20 on the NSE, down 5.03% or Rs 62.40 for the day. The fall made it one of the worst performers on benchmark indices as the IT pack remained under pressure.

Will petrol, diesel prices rise after elections? Here's what we knowThe government has dismissed rumours of a petrol and diesel price hike, but elevated crude prices and pressure on oil retailers have kept the debate firmly alive.

HCLTech, TCS, Infosys: Why are IT stocks falling again?HCLTech led losses as frontline IT stocks fell up nearly 5% amid renewed worries over global demand, weak guidance and continued foreign investor selling pressure.

Sensex closes 982 points lower: 3 reasons why stock market fell todayStock market crash: The BSE Sensex was down 1,069.89 points, or 1.38%, at 76,594.11 at around 1:08 pm, while the Nifty 50 fell 293.55 points, or 1.21%, to 23,879.50.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as oil surge, FPI selling weigh on marketsThe BSE Sensex fell 409.66 points, or 0.53%, to 77,254.34 as of 9:30 am. The NSE Nifty 50 also slipped 104.35 points, or 0.43%, to 24,068.70.

Zodiac Energy shares jump 10%: Why is the stock rising today?The rally appears to have been sparked by Zodiac Energy's recent disclosure that it has secured a Rs 30.85 crore solar EPC contract in Zambia.

entertainment.headline: Viral: Priyanka Chopra, Mahesh Babu's pic with Varanasi choreographer Bosco Martis Priyanka Chopra and Mahesh Babu's pictures with choreographer Bosco Martis from Varansi sets have emerged online. Reportedly, the duo has completed a dance sequence in Hyderabad.

Ahaan Panday talks of a possible collaboration with Aryan Khan Actor Ahaan Panday said he is open to working with director Aryan Khan in the future. However, he had a special condition that harks back to their childhood bond.

The body said the lapse, including his admitted contribution to the film's online leak, raised wider concerns.

Is Veronica back? Deepika Padukone's Cocktail 2 cameo rumours are trending A Reddit post has claimed actor Deepika Padukone may return as Veronica in Cocktail 2, sparking fresh buzz around the sequel. There is no official confirmation yet but the film has already garnered attention for its cast, music and vibe.

No China, no Gulf, Dhurandhar 2's box office supremacy still unabated after 5 weeks Dhurandhar: The Revenge has completed 36 days in theatres and is closing in on Baahubali 2's global total. Its run stands out because it has crossed Rs 1,766 crore without releases in China or the Gulf markets.

Row at Darling re-release show: Fan beaten with slippers, sticks. Viral videos The re-release of Prabhas' Darlings sparked a row after a man was beaten with sticks and slippers at a Hyderabad theatre. Several videos from the altercations have emerged online.

Prabhas' Darling records 2nd biggest Telugu re-release debut, earns Rs 7.59 crore Prabhas and Kajal Aggarwal's Darling has returned to theatres with its 4K re-release, with a smashing opening globally. The film now stands as the second-biggest Telugu re-release opener after Baahubali: The Epic.

She later posted nostalgic photos on Instagram, celebrating her journey as a devoted fan and the emotional milestone of performing alongside her idol.

Kunal Kapoor to make cameo in Ek Din, says Aamir's call was reason enough Actor Kunal Kapoor is set to make a guest appearance in Ek Din after his Rang De Basanti co-star Aamir Khan personally asked him to join the film. He described the romantic drama as warm and said he believes it will connect with audiences.

Priyanka Chopra makes history, to receive Global Vanguard Honor for 25-year legacy Priyanka Chopra will receive the inaugural Global Vanguard Honor for her unprecedented 25-year career across industries and continents. The ceremony will take place on May 9.

Sapne Vs Everyone Season 2: Khushalii Kumar makes her OTT debut with TVF show Khushalii Kumar announced her OTT debut with Sapne Vs Everyone Season 2, teaming up with The Viral Fever. She opened up about the collaboration, calling it a dream project as the next chapter of the series gears up for release.

His strategy combined cinematic appeal with digital innovation, aiming to make a lasting impact in Tamil Nadu politics.

Ginny Wedss Sunny 2 review: No magic, no logic. It's tragicRelease Date:Fri Apr 24, 2026 Ginny Wedss Sunny 2 movie review: Ginny Weds Sunny 2 attempts to explore arranged marriage and societal expectations but falters with a scattered narrative and clichéd storytelling, says our review.

Context matters: Rakul Preet rejects situationship narrative after Jackky's remark Actor Rakul Preet Singh responded after husband Jackky Bhagnani's "situationship" remark about their marriage sparked debate online. Calling it taken out of context, she explained what he really meant about their marriage and why nuance matters.

Felt like touching magic: Anupam Kher on meeting Michael Jackson, 'the 8th wonder' Actor Anupam Kher recalled his memory of meeting Michael Jackson in 1996 during his India trip. Kher, in a heartwarming note, called the pop icon the eighth wonder of the world.

Rakesh Bedi on Rs 1 crore Dhurandhar 2 bonus rumours: Hasn't reached my account Rakesh Bedi reacted to rumours claiming he received a Rs 1 crore bonus after the success of Dhurandhar 2 and said no such amount had reached him. The actor responded to the reports with humour while addressing the speculation.

Ahaan Panday, Aneet Padda hide faces, dodge paps at Amritsar airport. Watch Actors Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda were clicked avoiding paparazzi at the airport. The two covered their faces and maintained a low profile in a viral video.

Lawrence of Punjab: Centre halts OTT release of gangster docuseries after row The Centre has directed ZEE5 to halt the release of the controversial docuseries Lawrence of Punjab amid political and public outcry over its portrayal of gangster culture.

general news.headline: Raghav Chadha, Sandeep Pathak, Ashok Mittal join BJP after quitting AAPFollowing their bombshell exits from AAP, Rajya Sabha MPs Raghav Chadha, Ashok Mittal and Sandeep Pathak joined the BJP in the presence of party chief Nitin Nabin in Delhi on Friday.

Gave everything to Raghav Chadha, he landed in BJP's lap: AAP's stinging criticismAddressing a press conference in New Delhi, AAP MP Sanjay Singh accused the BJP of using the Enforcement Directorate and the CBI to poach seven of its MPs.

Ashok Mittal replaced Raghav Chadha, then joined him to dump AAPIn a big jolt to Arvind Kejriwal's AAP, Ashok Mittal – who replaced Raghav Chadha in Rajya Sabha – is among seven MPs who have merged with the BJP. The announcement was made by Chadha himself at a press conference on Friday.

The Secret Seven who left AAP high and dryRaghav Chadha, who had been with the AAP since its inception, and six Rajya Sabha MPs dealt a big blow to the party ahead of the 2027 Punjab Assembly elections by merging with the BJP.

Even before Bengal is over, Punjab has begun for BJPEven before the completion of the West Bengal election, the BJP's eyes are already locked on the Punjab 2027 polls. With Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha and six others crossing over from AAP, the BJP signals that its electoral juggernaut never powers down.
US right now is a failed state, well, almostEconomic might and robust internal security aside, Trump's America ticks enough boxes to qualify as a failed state in the political theatre of the absurd.

India weighs Chabahar Port stake shift as US sanctions waiver nears expiryAccording to Bloomberg, New Delhi is holding separate talks with the US and Iran on how to proceed on its $120 million investment in the port, underscoring the diplomatic tightrope India is walking.

India glows green from space in stunning Russian satellite images. See picsRussia's Roscosmos has released stunning new images of Earth taken by its Electro-L weather satellite, which orbits the planet from 36,000 kilometres above.

Why Israel is airlifting this 'lost tribe' from India's northeastFar from the Middle East, Israel undertook a silent mission in India. Announced last year by Benjamin Netanyahu, Israel on Thursday airlifted around 250 members of the Bnei Menashe community as part of its Operation Wings of Dawn.

In 2 months when you come to beg: Russia counters Poland's European Council tauntPolish PM Donald Tusk's remarks about Russia prompted a sharp response from one of Vladimir Putin's envoys, Kirill Dmitriev, amid Europe's jet fuel crunch. The exchange underscores how the EU's break from Russian energy has narrowed its options after the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

We sail or sink together: NSA Ajit Doval meets eminent Indian MuslimsNational Security Adviser Ajit Doval invited 14 eminent people from the Muslim community to the new PMO building. Doval heard their concerns and suggestions, and spoke about the path for a stronger and united India.

How Babur used village homes as a shield in the Battle of PanipatIt has been 500 years since the first Battle of Panipat was fought, and it is remembered mainly for the first use of cannons in India, and the beginning of the Mughal empire. However, some literature suggests that the conquest was built not only on gunpowder and cavalry, but on civilians caught between his and Ibrahim Lodi's armies.

Live: Punjab CM reportedly called Harbhajan many times before Chadha bombshellRaghav Chadha Quits AAP Live Updates: The public fallout between the AAP and its Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha witnessed a decisive escalation on Friday, when the parliamentarian, announced leaving the party alongside fellow MPs Sandeep Pathak and Ashok Mittal. Addressing a joint press conference, the trio said they were going to join the BJP. Moreover, Chadha said that nearly two-thirds of AAP's Rajya Sabha MPs have quit the party and will join the BJP as a faction. He said several Rajya Sabha MPs including disgruntled party leader Swati Maliwal and Harbhajan Singh have also quit AAP. The trio told the press gathering that seven out of 10 Rajya Sabha MPs of AAP have quit the party. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on Raghav Chadha and other AAP MPs' resignation from the party.

TMC took away her daughter: PM Modi at rally with RG Kar rape victim's motherPM Narendra Modi, addressing a rally in Bengal with the mother of the RG Kar rape victim, accused the TMC of failing to protect women and silencing their demands for justice.

Meta employee shake-up: layoffs, micro-management, new appraisal systemMeta employees are facing a mix of frequent layoffs, tighter work tracking, and a new appraisal system as the company doubles down on AI. The changes are reshaping how people work, perform, and even keep their jobs inside the company.

How Vijay's theatrics made his party TVK a viral force in Tamil Nadu pollsThalapathy Vijay campaigned for his first election as leader of TVK, using viral videos, robots and holograms to connect with voters. His strategy combined cinematic appeal with digital innovation, aiming to make a lasting impact in Tamil Nadu politics.

Live: Securing our interests key to ending ongoing war, says Iran officialUS-Iran War Live Updates: The situation in the Middle East remains tense as military activity and diplomatic efforts continue at the same time. The USS George HW Bush has arrived in the region, increasing the number of US aircraft carriers in operation there to three. Donald Trump has stated that any deal with Iran will be made only when it is "appropriate and good" for the United States. Iranian leaders, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, have rejected claims that there are divisions within the country's leadership. The US has taken further military steps. The US President has ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill" Iranian boats suspected of placing mines in the Strait of Hormuz. The US military has also intensified mine-clearing operations in the waterway. In addition, the Pentagon said US forces boarded the tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean. These developments come after the US extended a ceasefire with Iran, although there is no formal extension or confirmed timeline for further talks. Diplomatic efforts are continuing alongside military developments. The United States is hosting a second round of talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington. The discussions are focused on extending a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Both sides have carried out strikes during the truce period. So far, there is no clear deadline for ending the conflict or for reaching a new agreement. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

What makes this 100-year-old Parsi bakery in Pune so specialNo one was lying when they said old school charm rules. This century-old bakery has no artisanal desserts. They have no Gen Z vibe at all. And that's exactly their aesthetic that brings customers from across the city.

No rain in sight: India to feel intense summer heat on SaturdayHeatwave conditions are likely to continue over the plains of northwest and central India, including parts of Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh.

How killer of IRS officer's daughter used deceit to rape friend's wife in AlwarFocus has shifted to Alwar, where Rahul Meena allegedly used deception and familiarity to target a neighbour's wife hours before the Delhi crime. The survivor's husband said he learnt of the incident after his wife called him, saying Rahul had come and "did something wrong," breaking down during the call.

Complaint against Mumbai woman who berated minister over traffic jam due to protestOn Tuesday, an agitated woman confronted minister Mahajan, who was speaking to reporters, shouting at him for blocking roads and causing inconvenience to commuters.

No China, no Gulf, Dhurandhar 2's box office supremacy still unabated after 5 weeksDhurandhar: The Revenge has completed 36 days in theatres and is closing in on Baahubali 2's global total. Its run stands out because it has crossed Rs 1,766 crore without releases in China or the Gulf markets.

Why Raghav Chadha, once Arvind Kejriwal's blue-eyed boy, quit AAP after 15 yearsIn a major blow to AAP, Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha announced his exit from the party after 15 years and said he would join the BJP, along with six other parliamentarians. The move came amid a rift between Chadha and AAP after his removal as the party's Deputy Leader in the Rajya Sabha.

sports.headline: Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni gets six-match ban for abusing Vinicius Junior UEFA banned Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni for six matches for discriminatory abuse of Vinicius Junior during a Champions League clash.

Deepti Sharma's form a concern, but India keep faith in her before T20 World Cup SAW vs INDW, 4th T20I: Head coach Amol Muzumdar admitted that Deepti Sharma's dip in form is a concern, but backed her to bounce back. She has gone wicketless in her last five T20Is and has an economy rate of 10.73 on the tour of South Africa.

India submit Expression of Interest to host 2038 Asian Games India has submitted its EOI to host the 2038 Asian Games, with Ahmedabad emerging as a key contender, as the country pushes to host multiple global sporting events including the 2036 Olympics.

India must nurture Sooryavanshi if he is to play Test cricket: Harbhajan Singh Former India spinner Harbhajan Singh said India will need to handle teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi with patience and proper planning, emphasising that he must be carefully nurtured to become a Test cricketer.

Thomas Cup: Lakshya loses, but Ayush Shetty inspires India to 4-1 win vs Canada India secured a commanding 4-1 lead over Canada in their opening Thomas Cup tie, powered by wins from Ayush Shetty and both doubles pairs, including Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty. After Lakshya Sen's early loss, India bounced back strongly with dominant performances to seal the tie.

Does he have a passport? Cook's cheeky take on Sooryavanshi's India selection Former England captain Alastair Cook took a cheeky dig at the debate around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India selection, jokingly questioning whether the 15-year-old Rajasthan Royals opener even had a passport.

Struggling Suryakumar Yadav told not to be predictable by Wasim Jaffer IPL 2026: Wasim Jaffer believes Suryakumar Yadav has become too predictable in his shot selection, with opposition teams exploiting his strengths and restricting his scoring options, leading to a lack of consistency in the IPL. His struggles continued in Mumbai Indians' heavy loss to Chennai Super Kings, where despite a brief partnership with Tilak Varma, he failed to anchor the chase.

Vinod Kambli celebrates Sachin Tendulkar's birthday with kids at Shivaji ParkFormer India cricketer Vinod Kambli marked his childhood friend Sachin Tendulkar's birthday with a heartfelt gesture at Shivaji Park, where their cricketing journeys first took shape, as he shared ice cream with young aspiring cricketers.

Video: Akash Ambani walks out of Wankhede Stadium midway through MI's lossMumbai Indians owner Akash Ambani walked out of the Wankhede Stadium midway through MI's heavy defeat to Chennai Super Kings, a moment that reflected the growing frustration within the camp during IPL 2026.

Cake, fans, family and trouble maker: How Tendulkar celebrated his 53rd birthdaySachin Tendulkar celebrated his 53rd birthday with fans outside his Mumbai residence, where he cut cakes, interacted warmly with supporters, and thanked them for their wishes. The batting great shared a heartwarming family photo, featuring his mother, wife Anjali and a 'troublemaker'.

Called it? Akeal Hosein's Jadeja-like performance resurfaces old tweetIPL 2026: Akeal Hosein's four-wicket haul for CSK against MI at the Wankhede brought back an old tweet where he had expressed admiration for Ravindra Jadeja, drawing striking comparisons. Chinnaswamy Stadium in a high-stakes clash between Shubman Gill's side with struggling middle order and Rajat Patidar's side with vulnerable death bowling. MI explain debatable Mitchell Santner callIPL 2026, MI vs CSK: Mumbai Indians' decision to replace Mitchell Santner with Shardul Thakur as a concussion substitute has raised questions over the injury's nature and whether the move complied with IPL rules on like-for-like replacements.

Same track, same soil, should've batted better: Hardik Pandya after loss vs CSKMI vs CSK: Hardik Pandya admitted MI fell short in execution after a poor powerplay derailed their chase, insisting conditions were not to blame and that the team simply needed to bat better.

AI vs Human: Sony's table tennis robot 'Ace' outplays national-level playersA robot built by Sony has defeated elite human table tennis players in a breakthrough that researchers say could mark a new phase in robotics, particularly in tasks that demand speed, precision and real-time decision-making.
